items.content: NVDA reported Q1 2025 revenue of $26.0B, beating estimates of $24.6B. Data center revenue surged 427% YoY to $22.6B driven by AI infrastructure demand.
Jensen Huang announced next-gen Blackwell GPU architecture at GTC 2025, with major cloud providers committing to large-scale deployments.
US Commerce Department considering additional export restrictions on advanced AI chips to China, which could impact NVDA's international revenue.
Multiple analysts raised NVDA price targets: Morgan Stanley to $1000, Goldman Sachs to $950, citing continued AI spending cycle.
Semiconductor sector ETF (SMH) up 3.2% on the week. Broader market sentiment positive with S&P 500 at all-time highs.
